name: Connecticut SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
description: Connecticut's food assistance program providing monthly EBT benefits to purchase groceries. Administered by the Connecticut Department of Social Services (DSS).
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 185% FPL for most households in Connecticut

name: Connecticut HUSKY Health (Medicaid/CHIP)
description: Connecticut's Medicaid and CHIP program providing health coverage for eligible residents. HUSKY A covers children and parents; HUSKY B covers higher-income children; HUSKY C covers elderly/disabled; HUSKY D covers low-income adults.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly, and individuals with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL (HUSKY D); children up to 323% FPL (HUSKY B); pregnant women up to 263% FPL

name: Connecticut Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary wage replacement benefits to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by the Connecticut Department of Labor.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage/work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: Connecticut Housing Finance Authority (CHFA)
description: Provides affordable housing resources including down payment assistance, mortgage programs, rental housing development, and housing counseling for Connecticut residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; income limits based on AMI by town

name: Statewide Legal Services of Connecticut
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Connecticut residents statewide through a centralized hotline in matters including housing, family law, and benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals at or below 125% of the federal poverty level
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 125% FPL or below; some programs may have different limits

name: Connecticut Legal Services
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents outside the Hartford and New Haven areas in matters including housing, employment, and public benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals in the CLS service area
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 125% FPL or below

name: American Job Centers (CT Department of Labor)
description: Connecticut's workforce development centers offering job search assistance, training programs, resume help, and career counseling at locations across the state.
eligibilitySummary: All Connecticut residents seeking employment or career development services

name: Connecticut LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income households pay heating bills. Connecticut also offers additional state-funded energy assistance through Operation Fuel.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households with home energy costs, prioritizing elderly, disabled, and young children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 60% of state median income

name: Connecticut 211
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting Connecticut residents with local health and human services resources. Known as one of the nation's most comprehensive 211 systems.
eligibilitySummary: All Connecticut residents seeking assistance or referrals
languages: English, Spanish, and 170+ languages via interpreter

name: Connecticut Foodshare
description: Feeding America affiliate serving all of Connecticut. Distributes food through a network of 700+ partner programs including food pantries, community meals, and mobile pantries.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in Connecticut

name: Connecticut Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Assists Connecticut veterans and their families in obtaining federal and state benefits, operates a veterans home, and provides healthcare and residential services.
eligibilitySummary: Veterans, active duty service members, and their dependents residing in Connecticut

name: Connecticut Crisis Line (DMHAS)
description: Statewide crisis line providing immediate support for mental health emergencies, suicidal thoughts, and substance use crises through the Department of Mental Health and Addiction Services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Connecticut experiencing a mental health or substance use crisis

name: Care 4 Kids (CT Child Care Subsidy)
description: Connecticut's childcare assistance program helping low-income families pay for childcare so parents can work or attend school or job training.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children under age 13 who need childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 60% SMI initially

name: Connecticut TFA (Temporary Family Assistance / TANF)
description: Cash assistance program providing monthly benefits and supportive services to low-income families with children to help achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children who meet work or activity requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income thresholds; varies by family size

name: Connecticut WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children supplemental nutrition program providing healthy foods, nutrition education, and breastfeeding support for eligible participants.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children up to age 5
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% FPL

name: CTtransit
description: Public bus transit system serving Hartford, New Haven, Stamford, Waterbury, New Britain, Meriden, and other Connecticut cities with fixed-route and express services.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders; reduced fares for seniors, disabled, and Medicare cardholders

name: Operation Fuel
description: Connecticut-specific utility assistance program providing emergency energy assistance year-round when LIHEAP is closed, helping prevent utility shutoffs.
eligibilitySummary: Connecticut households facing energy emergencies, especially when other programs are not accepting applications
incomeLimitNotes: Generally up to 75% SMI; some flexibility for hardship cases

name: Connecticut Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes to reduce energy costs. Includes insulation, air sealing, and heating system improvements.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority for elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% FPL

name: Bureau of Rehabilitation Services (CT)
description: Provides vocational rehabilitation services for Connecticut residents with disabilities including job training, placement assistance, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Housing Authority of the City of Hartford
description: Provides affordable public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in Hartford.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Hartford area
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; generally at or below 80% AMI for admission

name: Elm City Communities (New Haven Housing Authority)
description: Provides affordable public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the New Haven area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the New Haven area
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; generally at or below 80% AMI for admission

name: Connecticut Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, shelter referrals, and support for victims of domestic violence through the CT Coalition Against Domestic Violence.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or affected by domestic violence in Connecticut
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter

name: Connecticut Paid Family and Medical Leave
description: Connecticut's paid leave program allowing workers to take paid time off for family bonding, serious health conditions, and other qualifying reasons.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who have earned at least $2,325 in the highest quarter of the base period

name: Roberta B. Willis Scholarship (CT)
description: Need-based state financial aid for Connecticut residents attending eligible in-state institutions. Awards vary based on financial need and institutional type.
eligibilitySummary: Connecticut residents attending eligible in-state colleges demonstrating financial need via FAFSA
incomeLimitNotes: Need-based; determined by FAFSA expected family contribution

name: Connecticut Adult Education
description: Provides free GED preparation, adult basic education, English as a Second Language, and citizenship preparation programs across Connecticut.
eligibilitySummary: Adults 17 and older who are not enrolled in school and need basic education or GED preparation

name: Metro-North Railroad (CT)
description: Commuter rail service connecting Connecticut cities along the New Haven Line to New York City, plus branch lines to Danbury, Waterbury, and New Canaan.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders; reduced fares for seniors and disabled
hours: Varies by line; generally 5:00 AM - 2:00 AM

name: Community Action Agencies of Connecticut
description: Network of community action agencies providing anti-poverty services including emergency assistance, energy assistance, housing, and workforce development.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families throughout Connecticut
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally 125-200% FPL

name: ConneCT Benefits Portal
description: Connecticut's online portal for applying to and managing DSS benefits including SNAP, TFA, Medicaid, and other assistance programs.
eligibilitySummary: All Connecticut residents can use this portal to apply for multiple assistance programs
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program

name: Connecticut Head Start
description: Federally funded early childhood education program promoting school readiness for children from birth to age 5 in low-income Connecticut families.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children birth to age 5 at or below federal poverty guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: 100% FPL or below; foster and homeless children automatically eligible

name: Connecticut Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounts on monthly telephone or broadband service for qualifying low-income Connecticut households to maintain access to essential communications.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal assistance program

name: New Haven Legal Assistance Association
description: Provides free civil legal services to low-income individuals in the greater New Haven area covering housing, public benefits, education, and immigration.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals in the greater New Haven area
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 125% FPL or below

name: CT Rental Assistance Program (RAP)
description: State-funded rental assistance program providing monthly subsidies to help low-income Connecticut families afford housing in the private market.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families and individuals who need rental assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 50% AMI
